parsed_topics.name: code_intelligence_layers
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the core architecture of Repowise, which builds multiple layers of intelligence (graph, git, documentation, decision) over a code repository to enhance AI agent understanding.

parsed_topics.name: git_co_change_analysis
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the use of Git history, specifically identifying 'co-change pairs' (files that change together) to uncover hidden dependencies and relationships within a codebase.

parsed_topics.name: dependency_graph_generation
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the creation of dependency graphs using tree-sitter to parse code and identify relationships between files and symbols, improving code navigation and understanding.

parsed_topics.name: decision_record_linking
parsed_topics.description: This topic addresses the linking of architectural decisions to code graph nodes and tracking their staleness, providing context and rationale for code changes.

parsed_topics.name: llm_context_optimization
parsed_topics.description: This topic highlights the potential for Repowise to reduce the number of tokens required for LLM queries by providing more targeted context based on code dependencies and change history.

parsed_topics.name: knowledge_wiki_generation
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the generation of an LLM-powered wiki with RAG capabilities, incorporating semantic search and freshness scoring to keep information up-to-date.

parsed_topics.name: memory_architecture
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the overall memory architecture of the system, including its layers, components, and integration with other repositories and models.

parsed_topics.name: vram_constraints
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the limitations imposed by the 12GB VRAM budget and how it influences architectural decisions and model selection.

parsed_topics.name: retrieval_strategy
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the retrieval mechanisms, including local-first approaches, the verdict protocol, and the integration of Claude for judgment.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: This topic details the Latent Topic Graph (LTG) concept, its implementation plan, and its role as a foundational substrate for various components.

parsed_topics.name: development_workflow
parsed_topics.description: This topic outlines the development practices, conventions, and tools used, such as bash wrappers, ref-indexing, and prompt decomposition.

parsed_topics.name: data_collection
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the process of collecting data for DPO training, including the verdict protocol and inference logs.

parsed_topics.name: prepared_artifact
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the concept of pre-compiling and indexing data sources into a persistent, queryable artifact, rather than directly querying raw sources.

parsed_topics.name: graph_exploitation
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes leveraging existing graph structures, such as wikilinks and code relationships, to enhance retrieval and reduce redundancy.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the architectural pattern of using hierarchical scoping (e.g., folders, rooms) to improve retrieval efficiency and relevance.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: This topic refers to the combination of different retrieval methods, specifically BM25, vector search, and graph traversal, to achieve more comprehensive results.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: This topic addresses the need to track superseding information and contradictions within the knowledge base to prevent stale or conflicting data from being used.

parsed_topics.name: behavioral_edges
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the use of behavioral edges, such as git co-change signals, to capture relationships between code changes and their impact.

parsed_topics.name: wiki_layer_architecture
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the design and implementation of a first-class wiki layer as a central component of the RAG architecture.

parsed_topics.name: infrastructure_setup
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the foundational elements of the local AI infrastructure, including upgrades, model installation, and baseline quality maximization.

parsed_topics.name: agent_persona_design
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the design and creation of specialized AI personas, including the architect persona for project bootstrapping and the security specialist for threat modeling.

parsed_topics.name: workflow_automation
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the automation of workflows, including the expense classifier, OpenClaw, and the idle-time runner for self-improvement and benchmarking.

parsed_topics.name: continuous_learning
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the memory and learning system, including data collection, distillation, and fine-tuning techniques to continuously improve local model quality.

parsed_topics.name: cross_cutting_techniques
parsed_topics.description: This topic outlines the core techniques and standards applied across all layers, such as structured output, few-shot examples, and the cascade pattern.

parsed_topics.name: persona_overview
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the purpose of personas, which are lightweight Ollama model configurations that pair a base model with role-specific parameters and a system prompt. It also introduces the two tiers of personas: Full and Bare.

parsed_topics.name: modelfile_structure
parsed_topics.description: This topic details the structure of a Modelfile, including the required and standard fields. It outlines the parameters and their purpose within the Modelfile.

parsed_topics.name: system_prompt_guidelines
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the guidelines for creating effective system prompts, including the required ROLE/CONSTRAINTS/FORMAT skeleton and rules for constraint creation.

parsed_topics.name: parameter_tuning
parsed_topics.description: This topic explains the purpose and recommended values for various parameters like temperature, top_p, and repeat_penalty, and when to use them.

parsed_topics.name: model_selection
parsed_topics.description: This topic provides guidance on selecting the appropriate base model for a persona based on the task and desired performance characteristics.

parsed_topics.name: persona_registration
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the process of registering a new persona with Ollama, including the command to use and the resulting lightweight manifest.

parsed_topics.name: bare_persona_guidelines
parsed_topics.description: This topic outlines the specific guidelines for creating bare personas, which are designed to be used with external tools that inject their own prompts and parameters.

parsed_topics.name: persona_creation_checklist
parsed_topics.description: This topic provides a checklist of steps to follow when creating a new persona, ensuring all necessary considerations are addressed.

parsed_topics.name: vram_budget_constraints
parsed_topics.description: This topic discusses the limitations imposed by the 12GB VRAM of the RTX 3060 GPU and how it influences architectural decisions, model selection, and context management.

parsed_topics.name: model_tier_performance
parsed_topics.description: This topic details the performance characteristics of different model tiers (8B, 14B, 27B, 30B) in terms of token speed, reliability, and suitability for various tasks.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_decomposition
parsed_topics.description: This topic explains the strategy of breaking down complex prompts into smaller, multi-stage prompts to improve reliability and avoid errors.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_architecture
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the architecture of the interconnected repositories (llm, expenses, web-research) and the role of the MCP bridge server in facilitating communication and consistency.

parsed_topics.name: dpo_data_collection
parsed_topics.description: This topic outlines the strategy for collecting data for Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) by logging model calls and incorporating human verdicts and automated quality scores.

parsed_topics.name: local_first_escalation
parsed_topics.description: This topic explains the approach of prioritizing local models for common tasks and escalating to a frontier model (Claude) for more complex or critical tasks.

parsed_topics.name: claude_code_integration
parsed_topics.description: This topic discusses the acquisition and analysis of Claude Code source code and its implications for integrating with the MCP server and understanding its functionality.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: This topic introduces the concept of a Latent Topic Graph (LTG), a novel retrieval substrate that prioritizes topic-level abstraction and non-contiguous content recognition.

parsed_topics.name: structured_output
parsed_topics.description: This topic emphasizes the importance of using grammar-constrained decoding (Ollama's `format` parameter) for generating reliable structured output, particularly JSON.

parsed_topics.name: pre_compile_wiki
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the architectural approach of pre-compiling sources into a persistent, interlinked markdown wiki before querying, rather than chunking and retrieving at query time. It emphasizes the value of an LLM-authored intermediate artifact.

parsed_topics.name: graph_exploitation
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the strategy of leveraging existing graph structures, such as wikilinks or code dependencies, to improve retrieval and knowledge organization, rather than building new graphs from scratch.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: This topic addresses the importance of tracking supersession and contradiction relationships between decisions or pieces of information, ensuring that outdated information is identified and replaced.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: This topic highlights the benefit of organizing information into hierarchical scopes (e.g., rooms, folders) to improve retrieval efficiency and relevance, as opposed to flat search spaces.

parsed_topics.name: code_signal_analysis
parsed_topics.description: This topic explores the use of behavioral signals, such as git co-change analysis, to identify relationships between code files and improve retrieval in code-related contexts.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the combination of different retrieval methods, such as BM25, vector search, and graph traversal, to achieve more comprehensive and accurate results.

parsed_topics.name: ollama_integration
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the integration with the Ollama LLM, including model selection, prompt construction, and handling LLM responses. It involves using the `ollama_chat` function and defining the `DESIGNER_MODEL` constant.

parsed_topics.name: persona_specification
parsed_topics.description: This topic focuses on the structure and content of the persona specification, including the JSON schema, available options, and validation rules. It defines the keys and constraints for the generated persona.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_engineering
parsed_topics.description: This topic deals with the creation of prompts for the LLM, including constructing initial prompts with codebase context and refinement prompts based on user feedback. It involves careful wording and formatting to guide the LLM's output.

parsed_topics.name: user_interaction
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the interaction with the user, including gathering the initial description, asking for refinement feedback, and confirming the persona creation. It uses functions like `ask` and `ask_confirm`.

parsed_topics.name: codebase_analysis
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the process of analyzing a codebase to extract relevant information for persona generation. It involves using the `detect` function and incorporating the results into the prompt.

parsed_topics.name: command_line_interface
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the command-line arguments and options for the script, including description, codebase path, dry-run mode, and JSON-only output. It defines the `parse_args` function and the command-line interface.

parsed_topics.description: Lists a checklist for creating a new persona, including steps and considerations.

parsed_topics.name: vram_budget_constraints
parsed_topics.description: Discussion of VRAM limitations and their impact on model architecture and performance.

parsed_topics.name: model_tier_findings
parsed_topics.description: Analysis of different model sizes (8B, 14B, 27B) and their performance characteristics.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_decomposition
parsed_topics.description: Strategy of breaking down complex tasks into multiple stages to fit within model capabilities.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Overview of the interconnected repositories and their roles in the overall system.

parsed_topics.name: dpo_data_collection_strategy
parsed_topics.description: Methodology for collecting and logging data for model fine-tuning through normal usage.

parsed_topics.name: local_first_with_frontier_escalation
parsed_topics.description: Policy of using local models for routine tasks and escalating to frontier models for complex tasks.

parsed_topics.name: claude_code_source
parsed_topics.description: Details about the Claude Code source code and its integration with the MCP server.

parsed_topics.name: smart_rag_research
parsed_topics.description: Investigation into advanced retrieval techniques beyond keyword/vector RAG for better content linking.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: Introduction of the Latent Topic Graph (LTG) concept and its distinguishing properties.

parsed_topics.name: structured_output
parsed_topics.description: Guidelines for using grammar-constrained decoding to ensure reliable structured output from models.

parsed_topics.name: retrieval_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Discussion of different retrieval architectures, focusing on 'retrieve-and-assemble' vs 'pre-compile-and-query' approaches.

parsed_topics.name: pre_compile_wiki
parsed_topics.description: Exploration of pre-compiling sources into a persistent interlinked markdown wiki for efficient querying.

parsed_topics.name: graph_based_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: Focus on retrieval methods that leverage graph structures, including wikilinks and typed knowledge graphs.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_search
parsed_topics.description: Discussion of hybrid search techniques combining BM25, vector search, and graph traversal.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Analysis of tracking supersession and contradiction in knowledge graphs.

parsed_topics.name: behavioral_signals
parsed_topics.description: Exploration of using behavioral signals, such as git co-change analysis, as edge types in knowledge graphs.

parsed_topics.name: architectural_amendments
parsed_topics.description: Suggestions for refining the architecture, including making the pre-compile step first-class and adding typed supersession links.

parsed_topics.name: decision_points
parsed_topics.description: Discussion of remaining decision points, such as whether to use one wiki per domain or a global one.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_construction
parsed_topics.description: Constructs prompts for the LLM to generate persona specifications.

parsed_topics.name: llm_interaction
parsed_topics.description: Handles interactions with the LLM, including calling the model and handling responses.

parsed_topics.name: spec_validation
parsed_topics.description: Validates the persona specification generated by the LLM to ensure it meets required criteria.

parsed_topics.name: display_proposal
parsed_topics.description: Displays the proposed persona specification to the user for review.

parsed_topics.name: handoff_to_creator
parsed_topics.description: Converts the validated persona specification into arguments for the create-persona.py script and runs it.

parsed_topics.name: main_flow
parsed_topics.description: Manages the overall flow of the persona building process, including user input, LLM interaction, and handoff to the creator script.

parsed_topics.name: argument_parsing
parsed_topics.description: Parses command-line arguments to configure the persona building process.

parsed_topics.name: interactive_mode
parsed_topics.description: Handles interactive mode where the script prompts the user for input if no description is provided.

parsed_topics.name: code_graph_intelligence
parsed_topics.description: The use of tree-sitter to parse code into a dependency graph with multiple tiers, including files, symbols, and call resolution with confidence scoring.

parsed_topics.name: git_co_change_analysis
parsed_topics.description: Analyzing recent commits to identify co-changing files that do not have import links, capturing cross-file dependencies missed by static analysis.

parsed_topics.name: decision_intelligence
parsed_topics.description: Linking architectural decisions to code graph nodes and tracking their staleness, enabling contextual awareness of design rationale.

parsed_topics.name: llm_wiki_generation
parsed_topics.description: Generating LLM-powered wikis with semantic search and freshness scoring to provide up-to-date documentation for codebases.

parsed_topics.name: code_context_enhancement
parsed_topics.description: Improving code editing by providing context from co-changing files, enabling more informed and less error-prone modifications.

parsed_topics.name: token_efficiency_optimization
parsed_topics.description: Reducing token usage by 27× through efficient retrieval of code context, avoiding naive context loading methods.

parsed_topics.name: existing_infrastructure_integration
parsed_topics.description: Leveraging existing tools like `ref:KEY`, Git history, and `benchmarks/` results to complement and extend repowise's capabilities.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: A conceptual framework for organizing and managing topics within the system, including named concepts and implementation plans.

parsed_topics.name: model_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Design decisions and constraints around model selection, including VRAM budget limits and context window ceilings.

parsed_topics.name: evaluation_framework
parsed_topics.description: A two-phase system for assessing model outputs, combining automated checks with LLM-based judgment.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_integration
parsed_topics.description: Strategies for maintaining consistency across multiple repositories, including MCP integration and shared scaffolding.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_engineering
parsed_topics.description: Techniques for structuring prompts to optimize model performance, including a validated 3-stage decomposition approach.

parsed_topics.name: retrieval_system
parsed_topics.description: A content-linking retrieval mechanism that connects multiple sources and philosophies, forming a central hub for information.

parsed_topics.name: system_constraints
parsed_topics.description: Hardware and resource limitations that shape architectural decisions, particularly the 12GB VRAM budget.

parsed_topics.name: content_linking
parsed_topics.description: Strategies for connecting different parts of content to enable relational retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: Combining BM25, vector, and graph-based methods for more robust content retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: precompiled_artifact
parsed_topics.description: Creating a static, pre-compiled knowledge base to simplify query processing.

parsed_topics.name: graph_exploitation
parsed_topics.description: Leveraging existing graph structures like `ref:KEY` for efficient content linking.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: Using nested or scoped memory structures to improve search efficiency and relevance.

parsed_topics.name: filter_before_fetch
parsed_topics.description: Using IDs or summaries to reduce the amount of data retrieved and improve context management.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Managing contradictions and outdated information through explicit supersession relationships.

parsed_topics.name: git_co_change_edges
parsed_topics.description: Capturing relationships between code changes to enhance context-aware retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: wiki_layer
parsed_topics.description: A central, pre-compiled knowledge base that serves as the foundation for retrieval systems.

parsed_topics.name: existing_infrastructure
parsed_topics.description: Utilizing existing tools and conventions like `ref:KEY` and `.memories/` for content linking.

parsed_topics.name: model_deployment
parsed_topics.description: Deployment of local AI models across different layers, including integration with Ollama and configuration of model parameters.

parsed_topics.name: evaluation_and_refinement
parsed_topics.description: Standardized evaluation of model outputs, feedback loops for improvement, and techniques for refining model performance.

parsed_topics.name: security_and_sandboxing
parsed_topics.description: Implementation of security measures, sandboxing environments, and access controls for AI systems, particularly in chat interfaces.

parsed_topics.name: cross_cutting_techniques
parsed_topics.description: Standardized techniques applied across all layers, such as structured output, few-shot examples, and temperature tuning.

parsed_topics.name: memory_and_learning
parsed_topics.description: Implementation of memory systems, distillation pipelines, and fine-tuning techniques to improve model performance over time.

parsed_topics.name: persona_configuration
parsed_topics.description: Defines the structure and required fields for creating a persona Modelfile, including base model, parameters, and system prompt.

parsed_topics.name: system_prompt_structure
parsed_topics.description: Explains the skeleton format for system prompts, including ROLE, CONSTRAINTS, and FORMAT, along with rules and examples.

parsed_topics.name: temperature_guidelines
parsed_topics.description: Provides temperature values and use cases for different persona types, such as accuracy, quality, and creativity.

parsed_topics.name: model_selection
parsed_topics.description: Guides the selection of base models based on the persona's role, including recommended models and their use cases.

parsed_topics.name: persona_naming_convention
parsed_topics.description: Describes the naming conventions for personas and Modelfiles, including how to name and register them.

parsed_topics.name: bare_persona_guidelines
parsed_topics.description: Outlines the requirements for bare personas, including no system prompt, minimal parameters, and comment-based rationale.

parsed_topics.name: persona_creation_checklist
parsed_topics.description: Lists steps and best practices for creating a new persona, including model selection, temperature settings, and system prompt writing.

parsed_topics.name: vram_budget_constraints
parsed_topics.description: Discusses the limitations and optimizations related to VRAM usage on consumer GPUs, particularly the RTX 3060 with 12GB VRAM.

parsed_topics.name: model_selection_criteria
parsed_topics.description: Outlines the performance and reliability metrics for different model tiers (8B, 14B, 27B, 30B MoE) and their suitability for specific tasks.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_decomposition
parsed_topics.description: Explains the benefits of breaking down complex tasks into multi-stage prompts to stay within the model's reliable output budget.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Describes the interconnected structure of three repositories (llm, expenses, web-research) and their shared hardware platform.

parsed_topics.name: dpo_data_collection
parsed_topics.description: Details the strategy for collecting labeled preference data through logging model calls and human verdicts for DPO training.

parsed_topics.name: local_first_with_frontier
parsed_topics.description: Outlines the default use of local models for routine tasks and escalation to Claude (frontier) models for complex or sensitive tasks.

parsed_topics.name: claude_code_integration
parsed_topics.description: Discusses the integration of Claude Code's source code and related repositories, including key files and tool-calling patterns.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: Introduces the concept of a Latent Topic Graph (LTG) as a retrieval substrate for semantic memory, combining topic-level abstraction and non-contiguous recognition.

parsed_topics.name: structured_output_decoding
parsed_topics.description: Explains the use of grammar-constrained decoding via Ollama's `format` parameter to ensure reliable structured output from models.

parsed_topics.name: pre_compiled_wiki
parsed_topics.description: The concept of pre-compiling sources into a persistent, interlinked markdown wiki for efficient querying, with typed knowledge graph layers and hybrid search methods.

parsed_topics.name: graph_based_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: Using existing graph structures (wikilinks, typed relations, co-change analysis) to enhance retrieval, rather than building new graph layers.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_search_strategies
parsed_topics.description: Combining BM25, vector search, and graph traversal for more comprehensive and context-aware retrieval, often as part of a pre-compiled wiki.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Tracking relationships between outdated and updated information (e.g., superseded-by, contradicts) to manage stale or conflicting data in memory and knowledge graphs.

parsed_topics.name: filter_before_fetch
parsed_topics.description: Using IDs or summaries to filter results before retrieval, reducing token usage and improving efficiency in context retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: domain_specific_wikis
parsed_topics.description: Creating separate wikis for different domains (e.g., llm, web-research, expense) to allow for domain-specific pre-compilation and retrieval rules.

parsed_topics.name: git_signal_analysis
parsed_topics.description: Using git co-change and dependency analysis to infer relationships between files, enhancing code-related retrieval and decision-making.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: Organizing information into hierarchical structures (e.g., rooms, wings, folders) to improve search efficiency and relevance before retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: llm_interaction
parsed_topics.description: Handles communication with the Ollama model to generate persona specifications.

parsed_topics.name: persona_specification
parsed_topics.description: Defines the structure and validation rules for the JSON output representing the generated persona.

parsed_topics.name: codebase_analysis
parsed_topics.description: Incorporates codebase context from the user's repository to inform persona design.

parsed_topics.name: user_refinement
parsed_topics.description: Allows the user to provide feedback and refine the generated persona specification.

parsed_topics.name: output_handoff
parsed_topics.description: Transfers the generated persona specification to the create-persona.py script for further processing.

parsed_topics.name: command_line_interface
parsed_topics.description: Implements the CLI for interacting with the persona builder, including argument parsing and execution flow.

parsed_topics.name: error_handling
parsed_topics.description: Includes mechanisms for handling errors during LLM calls, validation, and execution.
